budget: One bounded implementation card owned by the Claude lane. Promote the shadow ContextEnvelope to the single live prompt assembler (Linear META-19; the planned promotion of the META-4 shadow staging): add a section-based live assembly entry point in src/metaharness/context that accepts typed declared sections (section_type, source kind, trust, sensitivity, content) and deterministically produces both the chat-message list consumed by OpenAICompatWorker (harness/local.py) and the flat prompt consumed by CodingAgentWorker (harness/coding.py), so both worker families consume envelope sections rather than concatenating ad hoc. Enforce ContextTrust rules on the live path: UNTRUSTED_EVIDENCE and GENERATED_SUMMARY content can never occupy SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS, RESPONSE_CONTRACT, or TOOL_SCHEMAS instruction slots; evaluator receipts never carry INSTRUCTION trust; violations fail closed before any model call. Apply secret/credential redaction to the messages and prompts actually sent, not only the manifest; manifest entries must be derived from the same envelope that produced the live bytes so shadow/live divergence is impossible by construction. Journal the ContextManifest per attempt through the existing run-event sink as a non-shadow context.manifest event so 'what did the model see on attempt N' is answerable from the run journal; assembler failure remains fail-closed for contract violations and must not silently fall back to an unvalidated prompt for trust/redaction failures. The prior shadow-era byte-identity freeze is explicitly lifted by this card: live prompt bytes may change only as required by unified assembly, trust enforcement, and redaction, with tests attesting content parity otherwise. Existing META-4/5/6 test assertions may be updated only where they assert the superseded shadow-only contract, and each such update must cite this card. AMENDMENT v2 (coordinator, user-approved 2026-07-19, after independent codex plan review found the qualified scope cannot satisfy the acceptance boxes): owned paths expand to additionally include src/metaharness/workflows/journal.py (add context.manifest as an attempt-scoped canonical run-event kind so manifests reach the durable journal instead of being silently rejected), src/metaharness/core/types.py (additive Task.advice channel and additive WorkerResult.error_kind), src/metaharness/core/executor.py (carry accumulated reflections/knowledge hints as Task.advice rather than laundering them into boundaries; abort retries and exclude capability evidence for deterministic context-contract violations), src/metaharness/harness/runner.py (map LiveContextViolation to error_kind context_contract), and tests/test_run_events.py, tests/test_executor.py, tests/test_workflows.py. LiveAssembly must return every redacted transport surface (messages, tool schemas, flat prompt, system prompt) and adapters must consume only those returned values, with manifest surfaces extended additively (flat_prompt, system_prompt) and truthful per-adapter system-prompt attestation. All nine plan-review findings are folded into the build spec; no other scope change. AMENDMENT v3 (coordinator, 2026-07-19, after the frozen-diff panel): owned paths additionally include src/metaharness/workflows/engine.py, LIMITED to routing step boundaries that contain dynamic $steps.* output references into Task.advice instead of task boundaries (the workflow-channel variant of the F2 trust-laundering fix; codex panel finding 1). MCP tool-schema provenance (panel finding 3) is explicitly deferred to a follow-up card as a pre-existing condition.
stopCondition: Stop and fail closed on: any grant of evaluator, merge, promotion, deployment, credential, or self-approval authority; any expansion of specialist or worker runtime authority; H/E/W drift; expansion beyond the v2 owned-path set; a live message or prompt whose bytes are not derived from the validated envelope (shadow/live divergence); untrusted content accepted into an instruction slot; deletion or weakening of META-4/META-5/META-6 assertions other than superseded shadow-only byte-identity assertions updated with citation; nondeterministic assembly for identical inputs; a failing acceptance command; or an unresolved independent-review P0/P1 finding.
evaluatorAuthority: The implementing seat cannot self-approve. Coordinator integration and acceptance require immutable focused and full test evidence plus an independent adversarial review panel (GLM-5.2 frozen-diff per AGENTS.md, codex CLI read-only, Claude opus reviewer subagent) with no unresolved P0/P1 findings, and independent MiniMax-M3 behavioral verification with its own scratch probes driving trust enforcement, live redaction, both-builder envelope consumption, and per-attempt manifest journaling.
nextCheckpoint: A live section-based assembler exists in src/metaharness/context; harness/local.py and harness/coding.py both consume envelope sections; a test proves untrusted content cannot claim instruction trust on the live path; redaction demonstrably applies to sent bytes; the per-attempt context.manifest journal answers what the model saw on attempt N.
